mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and real estate investment, generating positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth consistently outpaces nominal GDP growth during a supply-constrained expansion, capacity utilization bottlenecks force price increases that boost corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during supply-constrained periods, or the outperformance of INDPRO over GDPC1 shows no correlation with 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent while the federal funds rate remains unchanged signals policy lag and rising real borrowing costs, triggering negative equity repricing over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered cases, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning from losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month payroll employment growth reflects immediate economic momentum and strengthens near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative mean following positive payroll revisions, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent during a late-cycle expansion signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and household balance sheet stress, driving negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing decline signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production crosses above its 12-month moving average, it confirms a sustained supply-side recovery that lifts corporate output and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of crossover events, or the signal fails to achieve a positive cumulative return in out-of-sample testing.
